title: potentilla arenaria subvar. glandulosissima
intro: Potentilla arenaria subvar. glandulosissima, also known as the glandular cinquefoil or sticky five-finger grass, is a variety of the Potentilla arenaria plant, which belongs to the rose family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat skin issues and wounds due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Potentilla arenaria subvar. glandulosissima include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to digestive problems.
